title: AutoStudio Editor Portal — Gap Completion
description: Implements the 6 missing pieces from the Editor Portal prompt. Do NOT rebuild anything already listed under 'already_built'. Work only on the phases below.
already_built: Ticket, TicketNote, EditorRating models and DB migrations
All admin editor API endpoints: list/create/update/delete tickets, manage editors, rate
Auto-ticket creation in studio.py when user processes photos
Email service functions: send_ticket_assignment_email, send_ticket_result_notification, send_ticket_status_update_email
Admin editor account creation endpoint and UI
constraint: Do not modify dashboard structure, visual branding, or any file not listed in a phase below. Do not push to GitHub.

phases.title: Backend: Add original_image_url and editor_result_url to ProjectOut schema
phases.why: ProjectOut currently omits these two fields so the frontend cannot display original or editor-corrected photos per project.
phases.change: In the ProjectOut Pydantic class (starts at line 19), add two new optional fields after 'image_url':

phases.title: Backend: ZIP download endpoint for editors
phases.why: Editors need to download all project files (original + AI result + edited result) as a single ZIP package. No zip endpoint exists anywhere in the codebase.

steps.action: Add 'import zipfile, io, os' to the top of editor.py if not already present
Add the following endpoint after the existing 'get_owner_logo' endpoint
code: """Stream a ZIP archive containing all files attached to this ticket."""
if current_user.role == Role.EDITOR and ticket.assigned_to_id != current_user.id:
# Map static URL paths to filesystem paths.
def url_to_path(url: str | None) -> Path | None:
return p if p.exists() else None
raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='No files available for this ticket')
steps.note: Ensure 'from pathlib import Path' is imported at the top of editor.py — it likely already is given ORIGINALS_DIR usage in studio.py, but add if missing.

phases.title: Backend: Email all editors when a new auto-ticket is created
phases.why: When studio.py auto-creates a ticket, no notification goes to editors. They currently have no way to know a new job is available unless they manually check the dashboard.

steps.action: Add the following function at the end of email.py
code: """Notify an editor that a new unclaimed ticket is available."""
A new editing job is available. Be the first to claim it!

steps.action: In the auto-ticket block, after 'db.commit()' (the line after 'db.add(auto_ticket)'), add editor broadcast notification. Find the block starting with '# Auto-create editor ticket' and insert after the commit/refresh:
steps.insert_after_line_containing: logger.info("Auto-created ticket %s for project %s", auto_ticket.id, proj.id)
code: # Notify all active editors that a new job is available
from app.models import Role as RoleEnum
steps.note: The User import is already at the top of studio.py. Check that 'is_disabled' column exists on User (it does — added in migration 423cc45866e8).

phases.title: Backend: Email user when ticket marked done
phases.why: When admin marks a ticket 'done', the customer (project owner) must receive an email that their edited photos are ready. Currently no user-facing completion email exists.

steps.action: Add the following function at the end of email.py
code: """Notify the project owner that their edited photos are ready."""
subject = f'Your edited photos are ready — {project_name}'
Your project {project_name} has been manually edited and is ready for download.
Log in to your dashboard to preview and download the final images.
text_body = f'Your edited photos for {project_name} are ready. View them at: {dashboard_url}'

steps.action: In admin_update_ticket, find the block that sets ticket.completed_at (around line 754-755). After 'ticket.completed_at = datetime.utcnow()' and before 'ticket.updated_at = datetime.utcnow()', insert user notification:
code: # Notify the project owner that their job is complete
from app.models import Project as ProjectModel

phases.title: Frontend: User dashboard Project Files section
phases.why: The user dashboard shows photos but has no dedicated 'Project Files' section with Original / AI Version / Edited Version tabs as required by the prompt. The ProjectOut schema now returns these fields (Phase A) but the UI doesn't use them.

steps.action: Find the Project type/interface defined near the top of the file (the local TypeScript type for project data returned by /api/projects). It currently has 'image_url' and 'result_image'. Add the two new fields:
steps.note: This type is defined inline in the dashboard page, not in a shared types file.

steps.action: Find where each project card renders its photo result (look for result_image being displayed in an or download button). BELOW the existing result image display, add a 'Project Files' collapsible section.

component_to_add.description: A three-tab section inside the project card: 'Original', 'AI Version', 'Edited Version'. Only render a tab if its URL exists. Each tab shows an preview and a download button.
code: /* Project Files tabs — add inside the project card, below the main result display */
{t('projectFiles') ?? 'Project Files'}

steps.action: Add the ProjectFileTabs component as a named function at the top of the file (after all imports, before the main page component). This is a self-contained client component.
code: const tabs: { key: FileTab; label: string; url: string | null }[] = [
const current = tabs.find((t) => t.key === active) ?? tabs[0];
className={`px-3 py-1 rounded-lg text-xs font-medium transition-colors ${ active === tab.key ? 'bg-[#CC2020] text-white' : 'bg-[#f5f5f7] text-[#555] hover:bg-[#ebebed]' }`}
className="absolute bottom-2 right-2 bg-black/60 text-white text-xs px-3 py-1 rounded-lg hover:bg-black/80 transition-colors"
steps.note: useState is already imported in the dashboard page. No new imports needed.

phases.title: Frontend: Editor ticket detail — ZIP download button
phases.why: Editors need a single button to download all files for a ticket as a ZIP. The backend endpoint is added in Phase B but no frontend triggers it.

steps.action: Add the following function at the end of the file
code: export async function downloadTicketZip(ticketId: number): Promise<void> {

steps.action: Import downloadTicketZip at the top of the file (add to the existing import from '@/lib/api/editor'). Then add a 'Download All Files (ZIP)' button in the file/actions section of the ticket detail page. Place it alongside or below the existing file download links.
button_to_add: className="flex items-center gap-2 px-4 py-2 bg-[#1a1a1a] text-white text-sm font-medium rounded-xl hover:bg-[#333] disabled:opacity-40 transition-colors"
steps.placement: Place the button in the file access area of the ticket detail. Look for where 'original_image_url' or 'ai_result_url' are rendered as links/buttons — add the ZIP button in the same section.

rebuild: docker compose build --no-cache frontend && docker compose up -d
verify: 1. Admin marks a ticket as 'done' → check that project owner receives completion email
2. User processes a photo → check that all active editors receive new-job notification email
3. Editor opens ticket detail → 'Download All Files (ZIP)' button appears and downloads a valid zip
4. User opens dashboard → project cards show Original / AI Version / Edited Version tabs (only for tabs with actual URLs)
5. ProjectOut API response for /api/projects now includes original_image_url and editor_result_url fields
